description: On a sandy beach, Joey lies on his stomach on a colorful towel, resting his head on his crossed arms.
As Joey remains resting on his hands, a voiceover from him says, "Okay, so we went to the beach because Phoebe found out about this lady who knew her mom and dad."
The setting changes to a room where Phoebe Sr., wearing a white nightgown, faces Phoebe.
Joey's voiceover says, "And I don't really know what happened with that." Phoebe Sr. says, "I'm your mother."
Phoebe says, "Oh." Joey's voiceover resumes: "And then Monica joked that she wouldn't go out"
Joey's voiceover continues, "with a guy like Chandler and he couldn't let it go," as a quick shot shows Chandler and Monica talking near a slatted door.
Over a shot of Rachel standing behind Bonnie sitting in a chair, Joey's voiceover adds, "And I don't really know what happened with that either. Oh, and then Ross's new"
Joey's voiceover continues, "girlfriend, Bonnie shows up and Rachel convinced her to shave her head."
"And then Ross and Rachel kiss. And now Ross has" says Joey's voiceover, over a shot of Ross and Rachel kissing.
"to choose between Rachel and the bald girl," Joey's voiceover concludes over a shot of Rachel and Bonnie standing near Ross.
On the beach, Joey lifts his head and speaks directly to the camera, "And I don't know what happened there either. You know what?"
Joey pushes himself up from the sand with his arms and says, "Hold on. Let me go get caught up."
The scene changes to the exterior of a two-story beach house at night, its windows glowing.
Inside, Ross walks down a hallway with blue walls, keeping his arms at his sides.
Ross stops at a closed wooden door, looks toward it, and opens it. Inside a bedroom, Rachel is massaging a green substance into Bonnie's scalp.
Ross steps into the room and says, "Hi." Rachel and Bonnie reply, "Hey."
Rachel continues rubbing the green substance onto Bonnie's head.
Bonnie looks at Ross and says, "Rachel is just helping me out. My head got all sunburned."
Ross grimaces slightly and tilts his head.
Bonnie turns her face toward Rachel and says, "Thanks a million."
Rachel pats her shoulders and replies, "Oh, you're welcome. Amelia,"
Bonnie stands up from the chair and steps toward Ross.
She leans her head toward him and says, "smell. It"
Ross leans forward and sniffs the top of her head.
Standing up straight, Ross says, "minty."
Bonnie smiles at Rachel before turning back to Ross.
Gesturing toward the hallway, Bonnie says, "Okay, I'll be in our room."
"Okay," Ross replies.
Bonnie walks out of the bedroom, pulling the door shut behind her.
Rachel wipes her hands on a white towel, sets it on the dresser, and folds her hands in front of her while looking at Ross.
Ross points a finger at Rachel as they step closer to each other. They wrap their arms around one another and kiss.
Rachel pulls her face back slightly and says, "Oh my God."
"I know," Ross says, keeping his hands on her.
They press their lips together again, maintaining a close embrace.
Ross steps back, breaking the embrace, and says, "Okay, I gotta go."
Rachel raises her hands slightly as Ross takes another step backward.
"I gotta go break up with Bonnie," Ross says, gesturing toward the door.
Rachel steps forward and asks, "Here?"
Rachel follows up with, "Now?"
"Well, yeah, I can't stay here all night," Ross replies, raising his hands.
"And if I go in there, she's gonna want to do stuff," Ross continues, pointing his thumb over his shoulder.
Rachel presses her lips together, keeping her eyes on him.
"Did you tell her that you are not in the mood?" Rachel asks. Ross answers, "No, she likes that."
Rachel looks down briefly and nods.
"Yeah, faking sleep doesn't work either. I can't tell you how many mornings I woke up with her," Ross says, gesturing with his hand.
Rachel interrupts with a sharp, "Okay."
Ross brings his hands together in front of his chest and says, "Yeah."
"Why am I telling you that?" Ross asks. "I don't know," Rachel responds with a slight smile.
Ross grasps Rachel's hands and pulls her in. They embrace and kiss once more.
Pulling his face away, Ross stammers, "It it"
Ross holds Rachel's arms for a moment before turning away and grabbing the doorknob.
"wasn't every morning," Ross says as he opens the door. Rachel raises her arms and replies, "Oh, making it worse."
Ross exits the room, closing the door. Rachel throws her arms up and drops them as the screen cuts to black.
The screen remains completely black in silence.
The setting changes to the exterior of a dark house at night.
Inside a kitchen, Phoebe Sr. asks Phoebe, "So I guess you would like to know how it all happened?"
Phoebe briefly touches her nose.
"I think I can figure it out. I guess I was born" Phoebe says, pointing at herself.
"and then everyone started lying their asses," she finishes, gesturing outward with her hand.
Phoebe Sr. turns and takes a step toward the dining area.
Turning back around, Phoebe Sr. says, "No, no, it wasn't like mean."
Walking back toward the kitchen, she continues, "Remember how I told you how Lily and Frank and I, we were close? Well, we were very close."
Phoebe crosses her arms and asks, "How close?"
"Well, the three of us, we were kind of a couple," Phoebe Sr. says, moving her hands in front of her.
Phoebe stares steadily at Phoebe Sr., keeping her arms crossed.

In a living room, Phoebe stands with her arms crossed, facing Phoebe Sr., who wears a patterned nightgown. Phoebe says, "I don't even know how that would work." Phoebe Sr. replies, "Well, we would-" before Phoebe interrupts, "I'm not asking."
Phoebe Sr. lowers her gaze slightly.
Looking back up, Phoebe Sr. says, "Well, anyhow, somehow I got pregnant."
"And I was scared. I was stupid and selfish-" Phoebe Sr. continues, her expression tense.
"-and I was 18 years old," Phoebe Sr. adds. "I mean, you remember what it was like to be 18 years old,"
"-don't you?" Phoebe Sr. finishes. Keeping her arms crossed, Phoebe replies, "Yeah."
Gesturing slightly with her head, Phoebe says, "Let's see, my mom had killed herself. And my dad had run off."
"And I was living in a gremlin with a guy named Cindy who talked to his hand," Phoebe continues. Phoebe Sr. sits down on a floral sofa.
Phoebe Sr. looks down, resting her hands in her lap.
Looking up at Phoebe, Phoebe Sr. says, "I'm so sorry. I thought I was leaving you with the best parents in the world."
"I didn't even hear about your-" Phoebe Sr. continues.
"-mom and dad till a couple of years ago," Phoebe Sr. finishes. "And by then, you were already grown up." Phoebe looks away briefly, her arms still crossed.
Phoebe Sr. gestures with one hand and says, "I don't know. You're here, and"
Phoebe Sr. raises both hands, palms facing up, while looking at Phoebe.
"-I would like to get to know you," Phoebe Sr. adds.
Phoebe stares steadily at Phoebe Sr.
"Yeah, well, everybody does," Phoebe replies, nodding once.
Phoebe maintains her stance with crossed arms.
"Really cool person," Phoebe says. Phoebe Sr. keeps her gaze on her.
"And you had 29 years to find that out, but you didn't even try," Phoebe adds.
Phoebe looks at Phoebe Sr. in silence.
"You know what? You walked out on me. And I'm going to do the same to you," Phoebe says, uncrossing her arms. As Phoebe turns away, Phoebe Sr. stands up and says, "Wait." Phoebe adds, "Okay?"
Phoebe begins walking toward the front door.
Opening the white front door, Phoebe says, "I don't ever want to see you again."
Phoebe steps outside, pulling the door shut behind her. Phoebe Sr. watches her leave.
The door reopens immediately. Phoebe steps back inside, covers her eyes with one hand, and asks, "Where's my purse?"
The setting changes to a dining area with sand on the floor. Joey and Chandler sit at a round table, while Monica stands in the background kitchen area.
Monica walks toward the table and says, "Shoot. We're out of soda." Standing up, Chandler says, "Oh, I'll go out and get you some."
Monica asks, "Really?" Chandler turns, gestures broadly with both hands, and says, "Nope. Because I'm not your boyfriend."
Monica smiles tightly. Phoebe walks into the room through a doorway, carrying a green purse on her shoulder.
Joey looks at Phoebe and asks, "Hey, Phoebes. How'd it go?"
Phoebe stops and faces the table.
"Well, my mom's friend, Phoebe is actually my birth mom," Phoebe says. Joey and Chandler stare at her in silence.
Monica watches Phoebe. Chandler reaches toward the table.
Holding up a small object with two fingers, Chandler says, "I found a dried up seahorse."
Phoebe looks at Chandler. Monica steps closer to the table.
Looking at Phoebe, Monica asks, "Sweetie, what are you talking about?"
"Oh, my new mom, who's a big fat abandoner," Phoebe says, walking past the table.
Chandler stands up, turning toward her, and asks, "Phoebe, wait a second. Don't you want to stay here and talk about-"
"-it?" Chandler finishes. Phoebe continues walking toward a hallway and replies, "No,"
Phoebe continues walking away from the table.
"-I need to be alone," Phoebe says as she disappears down the hallway.
Monica watches Phoebe leave, then turns back toward the kitchen.
Phoebe leans back around the corner and says, "Monica," and Monica walks after her. Chandler says, "oh,"
Chandler sits down and picks at an object on the table. Joey watches him.
Joey holds a chip and says, "man. Hey, what if you found out that your mom wasn't really your mom-"
"-and your new mom turned out to-" Joey continues, pointing the chip slightly.
"-be really hot?" Joey finishes.
Chandler turns to look at Joey.
Nodding and looking forward, Chandler says, "Oh, man."
Chandler continues nodding slightly, his gaze directed ahead.
Pointing a chip at Chandler, Joey says, "But it's your mom."
Chandler winces slightly.
Chandler recoils slightly and says, "Oh, talking about your mom," his pitch rising at the end.
Joey gestures with his hands, his mouth moving without audible speech. The scene changes to an exterior nighttime shot showing a full moon over the ocean.
The setting changes to a bedroom where Rachel lies on her stomach on a bed, writing in a notebook. Ross enters through the door, closing it behind him.
Ross says, "over," as he stands near the door.
Rachel gasps and begins to sit up.
Rachel quickly sits up on her knees and asks, "was it awful?" Ross steps forward and replies, "Well, it was long."
Ross continues stepping closer to the bed.
Stepping nearer to Rachel, Ross says, "I didn't even realize how late it was till I noticed the 05:00 shadow on her head."
Rachel covers her mouth with one hand and looks down.
Ross looks down briefly, then shifts his gaze back up to Rachel.
Clasping his hands in front of him, Ross says, "Anyway, she didn't want to stay. I called a cab. She just left."
Rachel looks at Ross, keeping her hands near her face. Ross maintains his gaze on her.
In a bedroom, Rachel sits on the edge of the bed while Ross kneels on the floor facing her.
Rachel lowers her hand from her mouth and shifts her gaze up to Ross.
Rachel reaches beside her, picks up a folded stack of papers, and hands them to Ross. "I wrote you a letter," she says.
Ross takes the papers.
Ross chuckles slightly and says, "Oh, thank you."
Audience laughter is heard as Ross holds the letter.
Ross smiles and says, "I like mail."
Rachel adjusts her sitting posture, looks down briefly, then shifts her gaze back to Ross.
Rachel says, "There's just some things I've been thinking about."
"Things about us," Rachel continues.
Maintaining eye contact, Rachel says, "And before you can even think about the two of us getting back together"
Rachel continues, "I just need to know how you feel about this stuff."
Ross looks down at the papers in his hand, then back up at Rachel.
Ross nods and says, "Okay."
Ross looks down at the thick stack of papers in his hands, then back at Rachel. Audience laughter is audible.
Gesturing with the papers, Ross says, "Well, it's it's 530 in the morning,"
Rachel smiles slightly.
Ross taps the papers against his open palm. "so I better get cracking on this baby," he says.
Ross stands up. Rachel also stands up from the bed.
"I'll be waiting for you," Rachel says.
Rachel adds, "Just come up when you're done, okay?" Ross begins flipping through the thick stack of papers as he says, "I'll be up in"
Ross flips through the pages.
Ross's eyes widen as he reaches the end of the stack. "18 pages," he says.
Rachel smiles and nods.
Ross turns the stack over and says, "front and back."
Rachel's smile widens.
Ross smiles slightly and says, "Very exciting."
Ross turns and walks toward the door, continuing to flip through the pages. Rachel sits back down on the bed.
The scene changes to an exterior shot of a beach house situated on sandy dunes under bright sunlight.
Inside the beach house kitchen, Ross is slumped over the island counter, his face resting directly on the open letter. He lifts his head, and the top page sticks to his face. He pulls it off.
Ross straightens up, rubs his face, and begins gathering the scattered pages of the letter.
Ross picks up his coffee mug, takes a drink, and immediately coughs, spitting back into the mug. He sets the mug down.
Ross slumps forward again, resting his head in his hands. Rachel walks into the kitchen from the hallway.
Rachel stops near the island counter and speaks to Ross, her hands by her sides.
Ross lifts his head, grabs the stack of papers, and holds them up toward her. "Done," he says.
Rachel places her hands on her hips.
Rachel asks, "You just finished?" Ross walks toward her, holding the papers.
"Well, I wanted to be thorough. I mean, this is clearly very," Ross replies.
Ross gestures between the two of them.
"important to you. To us," Ross continues.
Ross raises his hand slightly. "And so I wanted to read every word carefully."
"Twice," Ross adds.
Rachel places her hands on her chest, smiles broadly, and speaks briefly, leaving the sentence unfinished as she raises her eyebrows.
"Does it?" Rachel asks.
Ross's face remains still, his expression unchanging as he stares at her.
Ross slowly lowers his hands, his gaze remaining fixed on Rachel.
"Sorry?" Ross asks.
Rachel gestures with one hand, her smile fading slightly.
"Does it?" Rachel repeats.
Ross's eyes dart side to side.
"Does it?" Ross repeats softly.
Ross keeps his gaze fixed on Rachel.
"Does it?" Ross says again, his volume rising slightly.
Rachel nods. Ross looks down at the papers in his hand, then looks back up.
"Yeah, I wanted to give that whole does it part just another glance," Ross says, gesturing with his free hand.
"What are you talking about?" Rachel asks, her brows furrowing.

Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "Ross, you just said that you read it twice." Ross walks past her toward the dining area as Rachel continues, "Look, you know what? It either does or it doesn't. And if you have to"
Rachel continues, "even think about it" while Ross simultaneously says, "no, Rach," interrupting her.
Ross turns to face her, holding his hands out with his palms open. "No, I don't. I don't have to think about it. I don't. In fact," he says.
Ross points his index finger upward.
Ross brings his hands together and clasps them in front of his chest. "I've decided. I have decided that it" he says.
Ross stands with his hands clasped, looking at Rachel.
Rachel stands still, keeping her gaze on him.
"does" Ross says.
Ross maintains his clasped hands. Rachel keeps her gaze fixed on him, her eyebrows slightly raised.
"it," Ross adds.
Rachel looks down, pressing her lips together, then looks back up at Ross.
Ross stands in place, maintaining his clasped hands and nodding slowly.
In a living room, Rachel stands with her arms around Ross's neck while Ross holds her waist. They gaze at each other.
Rachel asks, "Are you sure?" Ross replies, "I'm sure." They embrace tightly.
They maintain their embrace. The setting changes to a sandy beach where waves roll ashore audibly in the background.
Chandler and Monica lie on towels on the sand. Chandler sits up slightly.
Chandler looks at Monica and says, "You all right? There's a nuclear holocaust." Monica looks toward him.
Chandler says, "I'm the last man on earth. Would you go out with me?" Monica makes a brief vocal noise, shaking her head.
Monica looks away from Chandler, pressing her lips together. Chandler gestures toward her.
Chandler points to his chest.
Monica smiles and looks back at him as he says, "I've got canned goods."
Joey walks toward Monica and Chandler from the water's edge.
Standing waist-deep in a large hole in the sand, Joey says, "Hey, guys. Take a look at this. Check this baby out. Dug me a hole." Monica and Chandler stand up and walk toward him.
Monica and Chandler stand at the edge of the hole, looking down at Joey.
Looking down into the hole, Chandler says, "Excellent hole, Joe."
Joey continues standing in the hole as a large wave approaches him from behind.
The wave crashes over Joey, filling the hole with water as he throws his arms up and says, "My hole."
Monica suddenly shrieks, grabbing her leg and hopping on one foot.
Chandler points at her and asks, "What is it?" as she continues hopping and crying out.
Monica hops on the sand, tightly gripping her upper leg and shouting loudly in pain.
Chandler steps closer to her, his arms extended, and says, "Can we help you?"
Chandler adds, "Wants to take you back to the house."
Monica continues hopping and clutching her leg.
Monica cries, "It's like 2 miles." Joey replies, "Yeah, and I'm a little tired from digging the hole."
Monica cries out loudly, leaning her weight onto Chandler's arm.
Monica points her finger forward and says, "Damn the jellyfish."
She shakes her finger, still leaning on Chandler.
Monica says, "Damn all the jellyfish. I've got to do something." Joey responds, "Well, there's really only one thing you can do." Monica asks, "What?"
Chandler asks, "What is it?" Joey replies, "You're going to have to pee on it."
Monica looks up at Joey with her eyes wide.
Monica asks, "What?"
Chandler pulls his head back, his eyes widening.
Chandler says, "Whoa."
Joey points to his chest, saying, "Don't blame me. I saw it on the Discovery Channel."
Chandler gestures toward Joey, adding, "You know what? He's right. There's something like ammonia in that that kills the"
Chandler finishes, "pain." Monica yells, "Well, forget it. Doesn't hurt that bad," then turns and screams loudly while clutching her leg.
Monica grimaces, her eyes squeezed shut in pain.
Joey places his hands on his hips and says, "You want some privacy? You can use my hole."
Monica continues clutching her leg. The setting changes to a living room with a sandy floor, where Phoebe walks in holding a bag.
Phoebe stops walking and says, "Well, I'm ready to get the hell out of"
Rachel and Ross sit together on a couch. Rachel holds Ross's hands against her chest.
Phoebe drops her jaw, looks at them, and says, "you. Are you? Oh, that's so great."
Rachel smiles widely and nods.
Phoebe says, "OOH. Not for Bonnie,"
Phoebe keeps her gaze on them.
Phoebe points at them and adds, "but for oh,"
Joey, Monica, and Chandler walk into the room slowly, avoiding eye contact. Monica wears a towel wrapped around her shoulders.
Ross looks toward them and says, "hey. How did the beach" Monica replies quickly, "nothing." Joey adds, "I don't know"
Monica briskly walks past the group toward the kitchen area, keeping her head lowered.
Ross asks, "what happened." Monica stops and replies, "Nothing."
Monica looks up toward the kitchen.
Monica says, "I'm going to take a shower." Joey replies, "Me too." Chandler adds, "Me too." All three walk away in different directions.
Rachel and Ross look confused. Phoebe stands near them.
Phoebe turns toward the door, saying, "Okay. I'm going to put this in the car." Rachel stands up quickly and says, "Oh, hi. To go check."
Rachel walks around the coffee table toward Ross, leaning in close to him.
Leaning near his face, Rachel says, "And it really does. It does."
Rachel moves closer, speaking softly, "It really and truly does," before giving him a quick kiss.
Rachel smiles and walks away. Ross watches her leave.
Ross picks up a thick stack of handwritten papers from the couch beside him, unfolding them.
Looking down at the pages, Ross mutters, "it."
Ross holds the thick stack of papers in both hands, flipping through the pages with a furrowed brow.
Ross continues to stare down at the papers, holding them firmly.
Ross pauses on one page, his eyes widening as he reads.
Ross drops the papers slightly and shouts loudly, his face tight with tension. The setting changes to a view of a city skyline under cloudy skies.

The view moves across the city buildings. The setting changes to the exterior of a coffee shop, with a sign reading "Central Perk" on the window. Inside, Chandler and Joey sit on a couch while Ross stands holding the papers.
Ross points at the papers in his hand and says, "She wants me to take her responsibility for everything that went wrong in our relationship."
Ross gestures widely with the stack of papers, saying, "I mean, she goes on for five pages." Chandler and Joey look up at him.
Ross lowers the papers, his jaw slightly open, as he stares forward.
Ross remains standing still with a blank expression. Chandler and Joey continue looking up at him from the couch.
